name: Columbiana County School Empl
description_short: NCUA-insured credit union in Lisbon, Ohio serving Columbiana County school employees with savings accounts, loans, and basic financial services.
description_long:
Columbiana County School Employees Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution headquartered in Lisbon, Ohio. Established to serve school employees in the region, it operates as a federally chartered credit union insured by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA). The institution focuses on providing accessible financial services to its membership base rather than pursuing broad market expansion.

The credit union offers traditional savings and loan services to eligible members. Their core offerings include share savings accounts with federal insurance protection, various loan products, and share certificates. Members benefit from NCUA insurance coverage up to at least $250,000 per account, backed by the full faith and credit of the United States Government. The institution maintains a physical office location in downtown Lisbon with standard business hours.

Columbiana County School Employees Credit Union distinguishes itself through its member-focused governance structure and narrow field of membership targeting school employees. Unlike larger financial institutions, it operates with a community-oriented board of directors that solicits member involvement. The credit union maintains conservative withdrawal notification requirements, requesting 7-10 business days advance notice for withdrawals of $15,000 or more from the same account in any one month.

This is a small, traditional credit union with limited online presence and basic service offerings. While it provides fundamental financial services and strong federal insurance protections, members should expect limited digital banking capabilities and a smaller product range compared to larger regional or national credit unions. The organization appears to prioritize stability and member relationships over competitive product innovation.
services: Board membership opportunities for qualified members
pros: Full NCUA federal insurance protection up to at least $250,000 per depositor
Backed by the full faith and credit of the United States Government
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure prioritizes member interests over profits
Solicits member participation in Board of Directors governance
Direct physical office location with established local presence since at least 2011
Serves specific community (Columbiana County school employees) with focused mission
Standard business hours (9am-5pm weekdays) for member access
cons: Very limited digital/online banking presence based on website
Restricted field of membership (school employees only) limits accessibility
Requires 7-10 business day advance notice for withdrawals over $15,000, unusual restriction
No indication of modern financial products or services (no PALs, credit cards, or investment services mentioned)
Minimal web content and outdated posts (2011, 2022) suggest limited technology investment
best_for: Columbiana County school district employees seeking basic savings and loan services
Members prioritizing federal insurance safety and community-focused banking relationships
Individuals comfortable with traditional banking and willing to visit physical branch locations
diagnosis: Best suited for Columbiana County school employees seeking straightforward savings and loan services with strong federal protection. Primary caveat: this institution appears to offer limited modern banking services and digital capabilities—members accustomed to online banking, mobile apps, or competitive loan products should evaluate alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
